revision.comment: 追加
revision.text:
'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町にある神社。
[[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。
元は、「信太社」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。
== 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田神比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、[[特選神名牒]]は、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。
== 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神)
== 脚注 ==

[[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。
元は、「信太社」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。
== 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田神比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、[[特選神名牒]]は、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。
== 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
== 脚注 ==

revision.comment: 追加
revision.text:
'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町にある神社。
[[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。
[[醍醐天皇]]より、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。
元は、「信太社」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。
== 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田神比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、[[特選神名牒]]・神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。
== 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
== 脚注 ==

'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町にある神社。
[[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。
[[醍醐天皇]]、延喜の制、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。
元は、「信太社」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。
== 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田神比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、[[特選神名牒]]・神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。
== 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
== 脚注 ==

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 菅田比賣神社 | 画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 1 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 伊豆能賣命(菅田比賣命) |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]
|創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月12日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町にある神社。 [[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。 [[醍醐天皇]]、延喜の制、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる神祇資料より。 元は、「信太社」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。 == 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田神比賣神社二座」と記されている{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}。また、[[特選神名牒]]・神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。 == 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神) == 脚注 ==

{{神社 |名称 = 菅田比賣神社 | 画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 1 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 伊豆能賣神 (菅田比賣神) |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]

|創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月12日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町にある神社。 [[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。 [[醍醐天皇]]、延喜の制、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる神祇資料より。 元は、「信太社」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。 == 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}。また、[[特選神名牒]]、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。だが、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかであろう。 == 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神) == 脚注 == {{DEFAULTSORT:すかたひめしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたひめしんしや]] == 関連項目 == [[大和国の式内社一覧]]

{{神社 |名称 = 菅田比賣神社 | 画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 1 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 伊豆能賣神 (菅田比賣神) |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]
|創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月12日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町(筒井城跡地)にある神社。 [[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。 [[醍醐天皇]]、延喜の制、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる神祇資料より。 元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。 == 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}。また、[[特選神名牒]]、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。 == 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神) == 脚注 == {{DEFAULTSORT:すかたひめしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたひめしんしや]] == 関連項目 == [[大和国の式内社一覧]]

元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

revision.comment: 23 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 菅田比賣神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 1 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 伊豆能賣神 (菅田比賣神) |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月12日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }}
'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町([[筒井城]]跡地)に[[鎮座]]する[[神社]]。
[[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。
[[醍醐天皇]]、延喜の制、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。
元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。
== 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、[[特選神名牒]]、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。
== 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神) *持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:すかたひめしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたひめしんしや]] == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] *[[筒井城]]

[[醍醐天皇]]、延喜の制、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。
元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。
== 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、[[特選神名牒]]、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。
== 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神) *持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:すかたひめしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたひめしんしや]] == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] *[[筒井城]]
==End Japanese==

{{wikidata link|Q134893097}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|菅田比売神社|version=106926975|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 菅田比賣神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 1 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 伊豆能賣神 (菅田比賣神) |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月12日 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }}
'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]筒井町([[筒井城]]跡地)に[[鎮座]]する[[神社]]。
[[添下郡]]の[[式内社]]で、[[菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|菅田神社]]と対をなす神社と考えられている。
[[醍醐天皇]]、延喜の制、[[祈年祭]]で鍬靱の[[幣]]に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。
元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。
== 概要 == 祭神は、結論からいうと[[伊豆能売神|伊豆能賣神]]である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式神明帳]]には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、[[特選神名牒]]、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・[[天目一箇神|天久斯麻比止都命]]を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、[[大和志|大和志料]]でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。
== 境内社 == *八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命) *春日神社(天児屋根命) *高良神社(武内宿禰) *加茂神社(事代主神) *稲荷神社(稲荷大明神) *持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] *[[筒井城]]
==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたひめしんしや]]

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

[[JA:菅田比売神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134893097}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 菅田比賣神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619
|緯度度 =  34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17
|経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 1
|ISO   = JP-29
|祭神   = 伊豆能賣神 (菅田比賣神)
|神体   = 
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}
|創建   = 不詳
|例祭   = 10月12日
|本殿   = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}}
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|神事   = 
|ラベル位置 = top
|地図   = Japan Nara
}}

'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}筒井町({{jalink|筒井城|Q11603011}}跡地)に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|Q134888232|菅田神社}}と対をなす神社と考えられている。

{{jalink|醍醐天皇|Q329753}}、延喜の制、{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}で鍬靱の{{jalink|幣}}に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。

元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。

=== 概要 ===
祭神は、結論からいうと{{jalink|伊豆能売神||伊豆能賣神}}である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式神明帳}}には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、{{jalink|大和志||大和志料}}でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。

=== 境内社 ===
*八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命)
*春日神社(天児屋根命)
*高良神社(武内宿禰)
*加茂神社(事代主神)
*稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
*持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>

[[JA:菅田比売神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134893097}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 菅田比賣神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619

|神体   =

|ラベル位置 = top

'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}筒井町({{jalink|筒井城|Q11603011}}跡地)に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|Q134888232|菅田神社}}と対をなす神社と考えられている。

{{jalink|醍醐天皇|Q329753}}、延喜の制、{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}で鍬靱の{{jalink|幣}}に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。

元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。

=== 概要 ===
祭神は、結論からいうと{{jalink|伊豆能売神||伊豆能賣神}}である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式神明帳}}には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、{{jalink|大和志||大和志料}}でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。

=== 境内社 ===
*八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命)
*春日神社(天児屋根命)
*高良神社(武内宿禰)
*加茂神社(事代主神)
*稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
*持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

[[JA:菅田比売神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 菅田比賣神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}}

|神体   =

|ラベル位置 = top

'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}筒井町({{jalink|筒井城|Q11603011}}跡地)に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|Q134888232|菅田神社}}と対をなす神社と考えられている。

{{jalink|醍醐天皇|Q329753}}、延喜の制、{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}で鍬靱の{{jalink|幣}}に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。

元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。

=== 概要 ===
祭神は、結論からいうと{{jalink|伊豆能売神||伊豆能賣神}}である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式神明帳}}には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、{{jalink|大和志||大和志料}}でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。

=== 境内社 ===
*八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命)
*春日神社(天児屋根命)
*高良神社(武内宿禰)
*加茂神社(事代主神)
*稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
*持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

[[JA:菅田比売神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134893097}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 菅田比賣神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619
|緯度度 =  34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17

|祭神   = 伊豆能賣神 (菅田比賣神)
|神体   =

|ラベル位置 = top

'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}筒井町({{jalink|筒井城|Q11603011}}跡地)に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|Q134888232|菅田神社}}と対をなす神社と考えられている。

{{jalink|醍醐天皇|Q329753}}、延喜の制、{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}で鍬靱の{{jalink|幣}}に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。

元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。

=== 概要 ===
祭神は、結論からいうと{{jalink|伊豆能売神||伊豆能賣神}}である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式神明帳}}には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、{{jalink|大和志||大和志料}}でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。

=== 境内社 ===
*八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命)
*春日神社(天児屋根命)
*高良神社(武内宿禰)
*加茂神社(事代主神)
*稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
*持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

[[JA:菅田比売神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134893097}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 菅田比賣神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|135|47|1|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619
|緯度度 =  34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 17
|経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 1
|ISO   = JP-29
|祭神   = 伊豆能賣神 (菅田比賣神)
|神体   = 
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}
|創建   = 不詳
|例祭   = 10月12日
|本殿   = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}}
|別名   =

|ラベル位置 = top
|地図   = Japan Nara

'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}筒井町({{jalink|筒井城|Q11603011}}跡地)に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|Q134888232|菅田神社}}と対をなす神社と考えられている。

{{jalink|醍醐天皇|Q329753}}、延喜の制、{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}で鍬靱の{{jalink|幣}}に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。

元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。

=== 概要 ===
祭神は、結論からいうと{{jalink|伊豆能売神||伊豆能賣神}}である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式神明帳}}には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、{{jalink|大和志||大和志料}}でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。

=== 境内社 ===
*八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命)
*春日神社(天児屋根命)
*高良神社(武内宿禰)
*加茂神社(事代主神)
*稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
*持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''菅田比売神社・菅田比賣神社'''(すがたひめじんじゃ)は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}筒井町({{jalink|筒井城|Q11603011}}跡地)に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|菅田神社 (大和郡山市)|Q134888232|菅田神社}}と対をなす神社と考えられている。

{{jalink|醍醐天皇|Q329753}}、延喜の制、{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}で鍬靱の{{jalink|幣}}に預かる<ref>神祇資料より</ref>。

元は、「篠田社(信田宮)」と称し、現在地の500m程北方の筒井小学校東側の北篠田と呼ばれる地に鎮座していたと伝わる。

=== 概要 ===
祭神は、結論からいうと{{jalink|伊豆能売神||伊豆能賣神}}である<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=添下郡 神社明細帳 108ページ |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。しかしながら、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式神明帳}}には、「菅田比賣神社二座」と記されている<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>。また、{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}、及び神祇志料では、菅田比賣神・{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}を祭神と記し<ref>「式内社の研究 第2巻」でも同様の祭神が記されている</ref>、{{jalink|大和志||大和志料}}でも、伊豆能賣神は疑わしいとしている。伊豆能賣神は、伊邪那岐が黄泉の国から戻って禊をしたときに生まれた神で、伊豆は『厳』で斎清める神。しかし、社名からも菅田比賣神を祀っていたことは明らかである。

=== 境内社 ===
*八幡神社(祭神:誉田別命)
*春日神社(天児屋根命)
*高良神社(武内宿禰)
*加茂神社(事代主神)
*稲荷神社(稲荷大明神)
*持佛堂(薬師如来坐像)<ref>郡山町史</ref>

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}} (site of {{jalink|Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011}}).

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Izunome-no-kami||Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}} (site of {{jalink|Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011}}).

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Izunome-no-kami||Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

== Japanese content ==

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}} (site of {{jalink|Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011}}).

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Izunome-no-kami||Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

== Japanese content ==

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}} (site of {{jalink|Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011}}).

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Izunome-no-kami||Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}} (site of {{jalink|Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011}}).

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Izunome-no-kami||Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}} (site of {{jalink|Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011}}).

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Izunome-no-kami||Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{神社

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011}}).

It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami||WD=|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami||WD=|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami||WD=|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
