'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sugatahime Shrine'''|菅田比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Sugatahime Shrine''' (すがたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Tsutsui-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} (site of {{ill|lt=Tsutsui Castle|Q11603011|WD=Q11603011|1=Tsutsui Castle}}).

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}, and is thought to form a paired shrine with {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)|Q134888232|WD=Q134888232|lt=Sugata Shrine|1=Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)}}.

Under the system of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}} and the Engi regulations, it received offerings of hoes and quivers in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}<ref>From Shinto ritual materials.</ref>.

It was formerly called “Shinoda-sha (Shinoda-no-miya)” and is said to have stood at Kita-Shinoda, about 500 m north of the current site, on the east side of Tsutsui Elementary School.

=== Overview ===
In conclusion, the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Izunome-no-kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Kami}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/65/?lang=0&mode=0&opkey=R166362381017073&idx=41 |title=Soekami District Shrine Register, p.108 |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. However, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmeichō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is written as “Sugatahime Shrine, two seats”<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-4-26}}</ref>. Also, in {{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} and in Shinto source materials, the deities are listed as Sugatahime-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Amekushimahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>The same deities are listed in ''Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 2''.</ref>, and in ''Yamato-shi'' as well, Izunome-no-Kami is treated as doubtful. Izunome-no-Kami is the deity born when Izanagi returned from Yomi and performed purification, with “Izu” meaning sacred purification. However, from the shrine name itself, it is clear that Sugatahime-no-Kami was worshiped.

=== Precinct shrines ===
*Hachiman Shrine (deity: Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto)
*Kasuga Shrine (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto)
*Kōra Shrine (Takeuchi no Sukune)
*Kamo Shrine (Kotoshironushi-no-Kami)
*Inari Shrine (Inari Daimyōjin)
*Jibutsudō Hall (seated Yakushi Nyorai image)<ref>''History of Kōriyama Town''</ref>
